message.content:
{
  "document_metadata": {
    "board": "CBSE",
    "subject": "Science",
    "class": "IX and X",
    "grade": "9, 10",
    "stream": "",
    "academic_year": "2025-26",
    "total_pages": "13"
  },
  "classes": [
    {
      "class_name": "IX",
      "subject": "Science",
      "part_or_section": "Annual Examination (80 Marks)",
      "units": [
        {
          "unit_id": "U1",
          "unit_name": "Matter - Its Nature and Behaviour",
          "marks": 25,
          "headings": [
            "Theme: Materials",
            "Unit I: Matter-Nature and Behaviour"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [
            "Matter in Our Surroundings",
            "Is Matter Around Us Pure",
            "Atoms and Molecules",
            "Structure of atom"
          ],
          "topics": [
            "Definition of matter; Particulate Nature of Matter; States of Matter (solid, liquid, gas); change of state (melting, freezing, evaporation, condensation, sublimation)",
            "Elements, compounds and mixtures. Heterogeneous and homogenous mixtures, colloids and suspensions",
            "Physical and chemical changes",
            "Atoms and molecules; Law of Chemical Combination; Chemical formulae; Atomic and molecular masses",
            "Sub-atomic particles (Electrons, protons, neutrons); Models of atom; Valency; Atomic Number and Mass Number; Isotopes and Isobars"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Absorption of heat in melting; Cooling by evaporation",
            "Pure and Impure substances",
            "Excluding separating the components of a mixture",
            "Bohr's model (implied via atomic structure)",
            "Atomic Number, Mass Number"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U2",
          "unit_name": "Organization in the Living World",
          "marks": 22,
          "headings": [
            "Theme: The World of the Living",
            "Unit II: Organization in the Living World"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [
            "Cell - Basic Unit of life",
            "Tissues, Organs, Organ System, Organism"
          ],
          "topics": [
            "Prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells; multicellular organisms",
            "Cell membrane and cell wall; cell organelles (chloroplast, mitochondria, vacuoles, endoplasmic reticulum, Golgi apparatus); nucleus; chromosomes",
            "Structure and functions of animal tissues (four types) and plant tissues (Meristematic and Permanent)",
            "Organs, Organ System, Organism"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Basic structure and number of chromosomes",
            "Cell membrane semi-permeability"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U3",
          "unit_name": "Motion, Force and Work",
          "marks": 27,
          "headings": [
            "Theme: Moving Things, People and Ideas",
            "Unit III: Motion, Force and Work"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [
            "Motion",
            "Force and Newton's laws",
            "Gravitation",
            "Floatation",
            "Work, Energy and Power",
            "Sound"
          ],
          "topics": [
            "Distance, displacement, velocity; uniform and non-uniform motion; acceleration; distance-time and velocity-time graphs; elementary idea of uniform circular motion",
            "Newton's Laws of Motion; Action and Reaction forces; Inertia (of a body and mass); Momentum; Force and Acceleration",
            "Universal Law of Gravitation; Gravity; Acceleration due to Gravity; Mass and Weight; Free fall",
            "Thrust and Pressure; Archimedes' Principle; Buoyancy",
            "Work done by a Force; Energy, power; Kinetic and Potential energy; Conservation of energy (excluding commercial unit)",
            "Nature of sound; propagation in various media; speed of sound; range of hearing; ultrasound; reflection of sound; echo"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Graphical representations",
            "Excluding separating components of mixture (in context of physical changes)"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U4",
          "unit_name": "Food Production",
          "marks": 6,
          "headings": [
            "Theme: Food",
            "Unit IV: Food Production"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [],
          "topics": [
            "Plant and animal breeding and selection for quality improvement and management",
            "Use of fertilizers and manures",
            "Protection from pests and diseases",
            "Organic farming"
          ],
          "subtopics": []
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "class_name": "X",
      "subject": "Science",
      "part_or_section": "Annual Examination (80 Marks)",
      "units": [
        {
          "unit_id": "U1",
          "unit_name": "Chemical Substances-Nature and Behaviour",
          "marks": 25,
          "headings": [
            "Theme: Materials",
            "Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [
            "Chemical Reactions and Equations",
            "Acids, Bases and Salts",
            "Metals and Non-metals",
            "Carbon and its Compounds"
          ],
          "topics": [
            "Types of chemical reactions: combination, decomposition, displacement, double displacement, precipitation; endothermic/exothermic; oxidation/reduction",
            "Definitions (H+ and OH- ions); indicators; neutralization; pH scale; importance of pH in everyday life; preparation/uses of Sodium Hydroxide, Bleaching powder, Baking soda, Washing soda, Plaster of Paris",
            "Properties of metals/non-metals; Reactivity series; Ionic compounds; Metallurgical processes; Corrosion and prevention",
            "Covalent bonds; Versatile nature of carbon; Hydrocarbons (saturated/unsaturated); Homologous series; Nomenclature (alkanes, alkenes, alkynes) with functional groups; Chemical properties (combustion, oxidation, addition, substitution)",
            "Ethanol and Ethanoic acid (properties and uses only); soaps and detergents"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Definition relating to logarithm not required for pH",
            "Halogen, alcohol, ketone, aldehyde functional groups"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U2",
          "unit_name": "World of Living",
          "marks": 25,
          "headings": [
            "Theme: The World of the Living",
            "Unit II: World of Living"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [
            "Life processes",
            "Control and co-ordination in animals and plants",
            "Reproduction",
            "Heredity and Evolution"
          ],
          "topics": [
            "Nutrition, respiration, transport and excretion in plants and animals",
            "Tropic movements; plant hormones; Nervous system (Voluntary, involuntary, reflex); Chemical co-ordination (animal hormones)",
            "Reproduction in animals/plants (asexual/sexual); reproductive health; family planning methods; Safe sex vs HIV/AIDS; Child bearing and women's health",
            "Heredity; Mendel's Laws of inheritance; Sex determination"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Brief introduction to evolution concepts implied in heredity section"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U3",
          "unit_name": "Natural Phenomena",
          "marks": 12,
          "headings": [
            "Theme: Natural Phenomena",
            "Unit III: Natural Phenomena"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [],
          "topics": [
            "Reflection of light by curved surfaces; Images formed by spherical mirrors (centre of curvature, principal axis, focus, focal length); Mirror formula and magnification (Derivation not required)",
            "Refraction; Laws of refraction; Refractive index",
            "Refraction through spherical lenses; Image formation; Lens formula and Magnification (Derivation not required); Power of a lens",
            "Functioning of human eye; defects of vision and corrections; applications of mirrors/lenses",
            "Prism, dispersion of light, scattering of light"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Excluding colour of the sun at sunrise/sunset for scattering topic"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U4",
          "unit_name": "Effects of Current",
          "marks": 13,
          "headings": [
            "Theme: How Things Work",
            "Unit IV: Effects of Current"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [],
          "topics": [
            "Electric current; potential difference; Ohm's law; Resistance and Resistivity; Factors affecting resistance",
            "Series and parallel combination of resistors (and applications)",
            "Heating effect of electric current (applications); Electric power; Interrelation between P, V, I, R",
            "Magnetic field; Field lines; Fields due to conductors/coils/solenoids; Force on current carrying conductor; Fleming's Left Hand Rule"
          ],
          "subtopics": [
            "Direct current and Alternating current (frequency); Advantage of AC over DC; Domestic electric circuits"
          ]
        },
        {
          "unit_id": "U5",
          "unit_name": "Natural Resources",
          "marks": 5,
          "headings": [
            "Theme: Natural Resources",
            "Unit V: Natural Resources"
          ],
          "possible_chapters": [],
          "topics": [
            "Eco-system; Environmental problems (Ozone depletion); Waste production and solutions",
            "Biodegradable and non-biodegradable substances"
          ],
          "subtopics": []
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "formative_only_content": [
    {
      "title": "Health and Diseases (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "The following topics are included in the syllabus but will be assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments. The reduces academic stress while ensuring meaningful learning. Schools can integrate these with existing chapters as they align well.",
      "related_unit": "Unit II: Organization in the Living World",
      "related_chapter": "Health and Diseases",
      "assessment_status": "formative"
    },
    {
      "title": "Elementary idea of conservation of Momentum (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "The following topic is included in the syllabus but will be assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments. The reduces academic stress while ensuring meaningful learning.",
      "related_unit": "Unit III: Motion, Force and Work",
      "related_chapter": "Force and Newton's laws / Conservation of Momentum",
      "assessment_status": "formative"
    }
  ],
  "practicals": [
    {
      "title": "Preparation of solutions (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "1. Preparation of: a) true solution of common salt, sugar and alum b) suspension of soil, chalk powder and fine sand c) colloidal solution of starch in water and egg albumin/milk",
      "related_unit": "Unit I",
      "related_chapter": "Matter-Nature and Behaviour"
    },
    {
      "title": "Distinguishing mixture vs compound (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "2. Preparation of a) A mixture b) A compound using iron filings and sulphur powder and distinguishing between these",
      "related_unit": "Unit I",
      "related_chapter": "Matter-Nature and Behaviour"
    },
    {
      "title": "Physical or chemical changes reactions (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "3. Perform the following reactions: Iron with copper sulphate; Burning of magnesium ribbon; Zinc with dilute sulphuric acid; Heating of copper sulphate crystals; Sodium sulphate with barium chloride",
      "related_unit": "Unit I",
      "related_chapter": "Matter-Nature and Behaviour"
    },
    {
      "title": "Stained temporary mounts (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "4. Preparation of stained temporary mounts of onion peel, human cheek cells & to record observations and draw their labeled diagrams",
      "related_unit": "Unit II",
      "related_chapter": "Organization in the Living World"
    },
    {
      "title": "Identification of tissues (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "5. Identification of Parenchyma, Collenchyma and Sclerenchyma tissues in plants; striped, smooth and cardiac muscle fibers and nerve cells",
      "related_unit": "Unit II",
      "related_chapter": "Organization in the Living World"
    },
    {
      "title": "Melting point of ice and boiling point of water (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "6. Determination of the melting point of ice and the boiling point of water.",
      "related_unit": "Unit I",
      "related_chapter": "Matter-Nature and Behaviour"
    },
    {
      "title": "Laws of reflection of sound (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "7. Verification of the laws of reflection of sound.",
      "related_unit": "Unit III",
      "related_chapter": "Motion, Force and Work"
    },
    {
      "title": "Density of solid (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "8. Determination of the density of solid (denser than water) by using a spring balance and a measuring cylinder.",
      "related_unit": "Unit III",
      "related_chapter": "Motion, Force and Work"
    },
    {
      "title": "Archimedes' Principle verification (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "9. Establishing the relation between the loss in weight of a solid when fully immersed in Tap water / Strongly salty water with the weight of water displaced",
      "related_unit": "Unit III",
      "related_chapter": "Motion, Force and Work"
    },
    {
      "title": "Speed of pulse (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "10. Determination of the speed of a pulse propagated through a stretched string/slinky.",
      "related_unit": "Unit III",
      "related_chapter": "Motion, Force and Work"
    },
    {
      "title": "Conservation of mass (Class IX)",
      "source_text": "11. Verification of the law of conservation of mass in a chemical reaction.",
      "related_unit": "Unit III",
      "related_chapter": "Motion, Force and Work"
    },
    {
      "title": "Finding pH (Class X)",
      "source_text": "1A. Finding the pH of Dilute Hydrochloric Acid, NaOH solution, Ethanoic Acid, Lemon juice, Water, Hydrogen Carbonate solution",
      "related_unit": "Unit I",
      "related_chapter": "Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour"
    },
    {
      "title": "Properties of acids and bases (Class X)",
      "source_text": "1B. Studying properties on reaction with Litmus, Zinc metal, Solid sodium carbonate",
      "related_unit": "Unit I",
      "related_chapter": "Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour"
    },
    {
      "title": "Types of reactions (Class X)",
      "source_text": "2. Performing: Action of water on quicklime; Heat on ferrous sulphate crystals; Iron nails in copper sulphate; Sodium sulphate with barium chloride",
      "related_unit": "Unit I",
      "related_chapter": "Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour"
    },
    {
      "title": "Reactivity series (Class X)",
      "source_text": "3. Observing action of Zn, Fe, Cu and Al on salt solutions; Arranging metals in decreasing order of reactivity",
      "related_unit": "Unit I",
      "related_chapter": "Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour"
    },
    {
      "title": "Ohm's Law verification (Class X)",
      "source_text": "4. Studying dependence of potential difference across a resistor on current; plotting V-I graph",
      "related_unit": "Unit IV",
      "related_chapter": "Effects of Current"
    },
    {
      "title": "Equivalent resistance (Class X)",
      "source_text": "5. Determination of equivalent resistance of two resistors in series and parallel.",
      "related_unit": "Unit IV",
      "related_chapter": "Effects of Current"
    },
    {
      "title": "Stomata mount (Class X)",
      "source_text": "6. Preparing a temporary mount of a leaf peel to show stomata.",
      "related_unit": "Unit II",
      "related_chapter": "World of Living"
    },
    {
      "title": "CO2 in respiration (Class X)",
      "source_text": "7. Experimentally show that carbon dioxide is given out during respiration.",
      "related_unit": "Unit II",
      "related_chapter": "World of Living"
    },
    {
      "title": "Properties of acetic acid (Class X)",
      "source_text": "8. Study properties: Odour, solubility in water, effect on litmus, reaction with Sodium Hydrogen Carbonate",
      "related_unit": "Unit I",
      "related_chapter": "Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour"
    },
    {
      "title": "Soap cleaning capacity (Class X)",
      "source_text": "9. Study of comparative cleaning capacity of soap in soft and hard water.",
      "related_unit": "Unit I",
      "related_chapter": "Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour"
    },
    {
      "title": "Focal length determination (Class X)",
      "source_text": "10. Determination of focal length of Concave mirror and Convex lens by obtaining image of distant object.",
      "related_unit": "Unit III",
      "related_chapter": "Natural Phenomena"
    },
    {
      "title": "Rectangular glass slab (Class X)",
      "source_text": "11. Tracing path of ray through rectangular glass slab; measuring angles of incidence, refraction and emergence.",
      "related_unit": "Unit III",
      "related_chapter": "Natural Phenomena"
    },
    {
      "title": "Binary fission and Budding (Class X)",
      "source_text": "12. Studying binary fission in Amoeba, budding in yeast and Hydra with prepared slides.",
      "related_unit": "Unit II",
      "related_chapter": "World of Living"
    },
    {
      "title": "Prism ray tracing (Class X)",
      "source_text": "13. Tracing path of rays through a glass prism.",
      "related_unit": "Unit III",
      "related_chapter": "Natural Phenomena"
    },
    {
      "title": "Embryo parts identification (Class X)",
      "source_text": "14. Identification of different parts of an embryo of a dicot seed.",
      "related_unit": "Unit II",
      "related_chapter": "World of Living"
    }
  ],
  "activities": [],
  "projects": [
    {
      "title": "Portfolio (Class IX/X)",
      "source_text": "For Internal Assessment: Portfolio that includes classwork and other sample of student's work.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    },
    {
      "title": "Projects/Posters/Presentations",
      "source_text": "Diverse methods of assessment... may include - short tests, oral test, quiz, concept maps, projects, posters, presentations and enquiry based scientific investigations etc.",
      "related_unit": "",
      "related_chapter": ""
    }
  ],
  "assessment_information": {
    "marks_distribution": [
      "Class IX: Unit I (25), Unit II (22), Unit III (27), Unit IV (6). Total Theory: 80. Internal Assessment: 20.",
      "Class X: Unit I (25), Unit II (25), Unit III (12), Unit IV (13), Unit V (5). Total Theory: 80. Internal Assessment: 20.",
      "Internal Assessment Breakdown: Periodic Tests (Best of two) - 5 marks; Diverse Methods/Projects/Presentations - 5 marks; Practical/Laboratory Work - 5 marks; Portfolio - 5 marks."
    ],
    "excluded_or_non_assessed_content": [
      "Separating the components of a mixture",
      "Definition relating to logarithm for pH scale (Class X)",
      "Derivation of Mirror formula and Lens formula (Class X)",
      "Commercial unit of Energy in Work, Energy and Power section (Class IX)",
      "Colour of the sun at sunrise and sunset (Scattering topic Class X)",
      "Information presented in NCERT text book boxes"
    ],
    "teacher_notes": [
      "The present syllabus has been designed around seven broad themes: Food; Materials; The World of the Living; How Things Work; Moving Things, People and Ideas; Natural Phenomenon and Natural Resources.",
      "Curricular Goals are interdependent, not separate curricular pieces of study. (Reference: National Curriculum Framework for School Education – 2023).",
      "The NCERT text books present information in boxes across the book. These help students to get conceptual clarity. However, the information in these boxes would not be assessed in the year-end examination.",
      "Practicals should be conducted alongside the concepts taught in theory classes.",
      "All practical work listed in the syllabus must be completed and maintained as a record by students."
    ]
  }
}
